texto: ...rather than ask why something happened (i.e. what caused it), Jung asked: What did it happen for? This same tendency appears in physics: Many modern physicists are now looking more for "connections" in nature than for causal laws (determinism).
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de M.L. von Franz que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “...rather than ask why something happened (i.e. what caused it), Jung asked: What did it happen f…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, jung, physics, com foco em termino e physics.

frase_original: ...rather than ask why something happened (i.e. what caused it), Jung asked: What did it happen for? This same tendency appears in physics: Many modern physicists are now looking more for "connections" in nature than for causal laws (determinism).

texto: As soon as we notice that certain types of event "like" to cluster together at certain times, we begin to understand the attitude of the Chinese, whose theories of medicine, philosophy, and even building are based on a "science" of meaningful coincidences. The classical Chinese texts did not ask what causes what, but rather what "likes" to occur with what.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a M.L. von Franz: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “As soon as we notice that certain types of event "like" to cluster together at certain times, we …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, eastern-philosophy, jung pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de jung e jungian.
